presentation.title: About Planet'Iris
presentation.p1: It all began with a fascination: the hidden beauty in every eye. Emilie and Denis founded Planet'Iris in 2024 in Avelgem, between Kortrijk and Mouscron, with a simple idea: reveal what the naked eye cannot see.
presentation.p2: Through iris macro photography, every session unveils the unique textures, colors and patterns of your gaze. The result is a breathtaking high-definition image: a true personalized work of art, whether for yourself, as a gift, or to immortalize a family moment.
presentation.p3: Because every iris is a unique imprint, just like your fingerprints, and that deserves to be seen.

vos-iris.title: Discover Your Unique Irises
vos-iris.desc: Did you know your iris is as unique as your fingerprints? With our macro lens, we get within millimetres of your eye to reveal every detail: the textures, the colour nuances, the patterns you've never really seen. The result will surprise you, guaranteed.

1.label: Being alone is sometimes better than bad company. And honestly, your iris speaks for itself.
1.alt: Photo of a single iris by Planet'Iris

2.label: Your other half also has a gorgeous iris? Time to find out and immortalize it.
2.alt: Photo of two irises by Planet'Iris

3.label: Two was good. Three is better. Especially when you can hang them on the wall.

4.label: Because the family resemblance shows in the eyes too. Spoiler: it's often striking.

6.label: Six irises, the whole family. Grandparents included. They deserve their spot on the wall.

tarif-section.delivery-desc: We deliver digital files within 3 business days and prints within 15 business days from receipt of the digital file.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before booking your session.
faq.q1: What happens during a photo session?
faq.a1: The session lasts about 30 minutes in our studio in Avelgem. No special preparation is needed. Our macro lens gets within a few millimetres of your eye to capture every detail of your iris: textures, colours, patterns, in the best conditions. We photograph both irises and you then choose based on your preference.
faq.q2: Does it hurt or is it dangerous?
faq.a2: Not at all. The device never touches your eye. Each shot lasts only a few seconds per eye. It's a contactless experience, accessible to everyone.
faq.q3: In which language is the session conducted?
faq.a3: Our sessions are conducted in French or English. To our Dutch-speaking friends, we sincerely apologise: we are doing our best to find the time to learn your beautiful language!
faq.q4: I wear contact lenses, what should I do?
faq.a4: You will need to remove your contact lenses before the session so that your iris is perfectly sharp in the photo. Plan to bring your glasses or come with bare eyes on the day of your appointment.
faq.q5: How long before I receive my photos?
faq.a5: High-definition digital files are delivered within 3 business days of your session. Prints are available within 15 business days from receipt of the digital file. The digital file is included with any print order.
faq.q6: What print formats and materials do you offer?
faq.a6: We offer two materials: acrylic (plexiglass) and alu-dibond (matte or glossy finish), in several ratios (square, rectangle, round). Dimensions vary by format. Check our Pricing page for full details.
faq.a7: Yes, children are welcome. A supplement of €35 per child under 12 applies, due to the extra care needed to focus on a moving iris.
faq.a8: Yes! A supplement of €65 per animal applies. For pets, we travel to your home. Travel is free within a 10 km radius around Avelgem (20 km return included). Beyond that, travel costs of €0.50 per kilometre are charged. Please contact us beforehand via our social media to check feasibility depending on the type of animal.
faq.a9: Our studio is located in Avelgem (8580), Belgium, between Kortrijk and Mouscron. The exact address will be communicated upon confirmation of your appointment.
faq.q10: How do I book a session?
faq.a10: Booking is done exclusively via private message on our social media: Facebook (planet.iris.belgique) or Instagram (planet_iris_belgique). We will reply as soon as possible to arrange a date.

children.label: Children
children.desc: Available from any age, though we recommend a minimum of 5 years for best results (supplement of €35). Due to varying reactions to light, patience and stability required, we cannot guarantee a result. If a photo cannot be taken, the supplement remains due for the time spent, but the iris will not be charged.

animals.label: Pets
animals.desc: Available with a supplement of €65 per animal. Please contact us beforehand to check feasibility depending on the type of animal.

custom.label: Custom composition
custom.desc: If none of our models suit you, we can create a custom composition on the day of your session: iris arrangement, shard density, overall placement. Supplement of €20.

tarifs-page.digital-desc: Delivery within 3 business days after the session.
tarifs-page.print-desc: Delivery within 15 business days after receipt of the digital file.
tarifs-page.extra-iris: Beyond 6 iris, each additional iris is charged +€10. Applies to both digital files and prints.

politique.intro-desc: Welcome to Planet'Iris. We take the privacy of your personal data very seriously. This privacy policy explains what information we collect, how we use it and how we protect it.

politique.collect-li1: Personal information: when you use our website, we may collect information such as your name, email address, and any other information you voluntarily provide.

politique.use-desc: We use the information collected for the following purposes:
politique.use-li2: Personalise your experience on our website
politique.use-li3: Analyse website usage and improve its performance
politique.use-li4: Send marketing communications if you have consented
politique.sharing-desc: We do not sell, trade or transfer your personal information to third parties without your consent, except for the purposes described in this policy.
politique.ga-desc: We use Google Analytics to collect information about the use of our website. You can prevent Google Analytics from recognising you on future visits by disabling cookies in your browser.
politique.security-desc: We implement a variety of security measures to maintain the safety of your personal information.

politique.rights-li4: Restrict the processing of your personal data
politique.rights-li5: Object to the processing of your personal data
politique.rights-li6: Request portability of your personal data

page-title.article-marrons: Brown eyes in iris photography: a stunning result | Planet'Iris

blog-page.hero-desc: Tips, info and inspiration around iris photography.

iris-yeux-marrons.desc: Think your brown eyes will look plain in a photo? It's one of the most common misconceptions. In macro, brown irises reveal textures and colours you never imagined.

photo-iris-cataracte.desc: Have a cataract? Discover why an iris photography session is perfectly feasible at Planet'Iris, with short exposures per eye and no contact with the eye.

article-teaser.title: Iris & cataracts: is a session possible?

article-cataracte.hero-title: Iris photography and cataracts: it's possible
article-cataracte.hero-intro: Have a cataract, or a loved one concerned? Good news: in the vast majority of cases, an iris photography session at Planet'Iris is perfectly feasible. Here's everything you need to know.
article-cataracte.s1-p1: A cataract is a clouding that forms on the lens of the eye. It does not affect the iris itself, which remains perfectly visible and photographable. Our macro camera captures the textures, reliefs and colours of the iris, details located well in front of the lens.
article-cataracte.s1-p2: In other words: even if your vision is affected by cataracts, your iris is intact and its beauty can be immortalised without any issue.
article-cataracte.s2-title: Intense lighting, short exposures per eye
article-cataracte.s2-p1: This is often the first concern: "Won't the light bother my eye?" To reveal every detail of the iris, the lighting used is indeed intense, that's a technical necessity. But here's what matters: each exposure per eye lasts only a few seconds. The shot is repeated to achieve the best result, but the total cumulative time under the light remains short and fully controlled.
article-cataracte.s2-p2: We work at your pace and take breaks between shots if your eye is particularly sensitive. The goal is to get the best possible image while keeping the total exposure time to a minimum.
article-cataracte.s3-title: A session with no contact, no risk
article-cataracte.s3-p1: Our camera never touches your eye. The shot is taken from a few millimetres away, in a calm, relaxed environment. There's nothing particular you need to do, just look straight ahead.
article-cataracte.s3-p2: If you have a particular sensitivity, prescribed eye drops or wear contact lenses, let us know before your session. We always adapt our approach to your individual situation.
article-cataracte.s4-p1: If you have recently had cataract surgery, it is advisable to wait until your eye is fully healed before booking a session, generally several weeks after the operation. Your ophthalmologist is best placed to give you the green light.
article-cataracte.s4-p2: Once recovered, your iris is just as photographable, and often reveals even more fascinating details.
article-cataracte.cta-desc: Have questions about your personal situation? Contact us, we'll be happy to answer with no commitment.
article-cataracte.meta-desc: Have a cataract? An iris photography session at Planet'Iris is entirely feasible. No contact, controlled lighting, and a guaranteed result.

article-marrons.hero-title: Brown eyes: far more beautiful than you think
article-marrons.hero-intro: You think your brown eyes will look plain in a photo? It's one of the most common misconceptions among our clients, and one of the most wrong. Brown irises are, in most cases, absolutely fascinating in macro photography.
article-marrons.s1-p1: Clients often arrive saying: "I'm not sure my eyes are worth it, they're just brown." It's understandable: to the naked eye, the brown iris can look plain, even dull. But what the naked eye can't see is precisely what our macro camera reveals.
article-marrons.s1-p2: The reality is often the complete opposite. Brown eyes are among our most spectacular results, and the reaction at delivery is almost always the same: "I didn't know my eyes looked like that."
article-marrons.s2-p1: If we had to sum up brown irises in one image, it would be a blend of spices: cinnamon, cumin, saffron, Sichuan pepper. The iris fibres, seen in macro, form dense and complex reliefs, with tonal variations that the naked eye simply cannot perceive.
article-marrons.s2-p2: Some brown irises feature amber, caramel or even copper zones. Others have hazel tones that shift to gold depending on the lighting. It's this density of material that makes macro photography of a brown iris so special.
article-marrons.s3-p1: What surprises most is the discovery of completely unexpected colours. Under macro lighting, russet, orange, and sometimes slightly greenish tones appear in irises their owners simply called "brown". This isn't a retouching effect: it's the reality of your iris, finally visible.
article-marrons.s3-p2: The pigmentation of the brown iris is actually among the richest that exists. Melanin concentrates densely and unevenly, creating those unique patterns that only a quality macro lens can faithfully capture.
article-marrons.s4-title: The final result will surprise you
article-marrons.s4-p1: A brown iris photographed in macro is a work of art in its own right. The details are sharp, the textures deep, the colours warm and rich. Whether as a digital file or printed on acrylic or alu-dibond, the result is always striking.
article-marrons.s4-p2: If you're still hesitating, just come with your doubts. We'll let the final photo speak for itself.
article-marrons.cta-desc: Whatever colour your eyes are, every iris is a unique work of art. Contact us to book your session in Avelgem.
article-marrons.meta-desc: Think your brown eyes won't look great in a photo? In macro photography, brown irises are among our most spectacular results. Discover why.
